post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 288: Gemstone Mafia

Important Rules:
Do not edit or delete posts
Do not post during night
Do not post if you are dead or if you are not in the game
Do not talk about anything related to this game outside this thread
Read all the rules!


Rules
  • Rules
    • 1.1: Read the rules. All of them. Read them multiple times if necessary.
    • 1.2: Do not post at night.
    • 1.3: You may PM me at any time and ask me anything, such as rule clarification.
    • 1.4: Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the mods in charge, unless I specifically allow you to do so.
    • 1.5: You may not use any formatting that has an effect of concealing text. This includes size=1, hide="", color=#FFFFFF, and color=white, among others. This does not include adding extra spaces.
    • 1.6: Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted; this includes editing right after you post (what goes in the thread stays in the thread). If you for any reason find the need to change your post, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
    • 1.7: If you are not a living participant of the game, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread.
    • 1.8: Do not advocate for breaking the rules.
    • 1.9: Do not use the colors red (#FF0000), blue (#0000FF), green (#00FF00), or anything that appears similar, as these colors are used for modding purposes.
    • 1.10: Confirm your participation in the game by making a post stating the important rules
  • Voting/Hanging
    • 2.1: Post votes in code tags in the format 
      [code]Vote: <insert player here>[/‎code]

      Since AoPS does not distinguish between usernames with different capitalizations, the capitalization of the player name does not matter. A proper vote looks like 
      Vote: fdas
    • 2.2: Votes may not be placed in hide tags.
    • 2.3: Improperly made votes will not be counted under any circumstances. It does not matter if it is the end of the game hammer and everyone revealed their roles. Hint: don’t reveal your roles and declare victory until I made the official end of the game post.
    • 2.4: You may vote for no hang to occur during a day. Vote this by including in your post 
      Vote: No Hang
    • 2.5: You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, though it is appreciated if you do so. Unvote in the format 
      Vote: Unvote

      An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
    • 2.6: Spell the username of the player you wish to vote for exactly. Do not use nicknames.
    • 2.7: If at any point in a day more than half of the votes are for a single player, that player will be hanged. It will then immediately be twilight.
    • 2.8: If at any point in a day at least half of the votes are for no hang, then there will be no hang. It will then be twilight.
    • 2.9: If the deadline is met and no person is hanged, it will be considered no hang and will proceed to be twilight.
    • 2.10: The moment the deadline passes, no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post.
    • 2.11: During twilight, players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
    • 2.12: Once I post a end-of-day scene, the person hanged (if any) is officially dead. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.
  • Activity
    • 3.1: Each day will be 336 hours (2 weeks). The moderator may extend the deadline for the end of the day at his/her discretion. Note that this will be unlikely.
    • 3.2: The deadlines will follow the official time
    • 3.3: The deadline will occur the very second 336 hours has occurred.
    • 3.4: The night deadline will be 72 hours after night begins.
    • 3.5: When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from either a player or his/her partner(s) (if you have them), the player will lose the chance to perform an action during that night.
    • 3.6: Any night results will be sent after the night deadline
    • 3.7: Night will end at some time after the night deadline when the mod makes the post announcing the end of night.
    • 3.8: If a player does not post in a 48-hour period and he/she is not in V/LA, you will receive an in-game prod.
    • 3.9: If a player does not post in another 24-hour period after receiving an in-game prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will receive a PM prod.
    • 3.10: If a player does not post in another 48-hour period after receiving a PM prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will be replaced, unless you post before the moderator finds a replacement.
    • 3.11: If a player receives 5 PM Prods, he/she will be replaced
    • 3.12: If a player is going to be V/LA, he/she must post in bold in-thread or PM me. The moderator will not prod the player unless the V/LA is too long.

  • Etiquette
    • 4.1: Do not use inappropriate language.
    • 4.2: Do not make any personal attacks.
    • 4.3: Follow all of the rules.
    • 4.4: Be mature.
    • 4.5: For a minor rule infraction, players can receive up to two warnings. If a player commits an infraction after he/she has run out of warnings, or commits a modkill level infraction, he/she will be modkilled or force replaced. Please do not make me modkill you.

  • Moderators/Co-Moderators
    • 5.1: I fdas am the moderator of Mafia Game 288 and BananaBall00 is the co mod
    • 5.2: Red-colored text (#FF0000) is used for official votecounts and activity checks by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related posts.
    • 5.3: Blue-colored text (#0000FF) is used for the moderator and co-mod's posts other than votecounts and activity checks.
    • 5.4: Green-colored text (#00FF00) is used for town flavor-related posts.
    • 5.5: The not voting section is to be ignored if it contradicts any other part of the votecount
    • 5.6: If an error to the votecount is not pointed out within 5 days, the incorrect votecount will become official.
    • 5.7: The moderator may change the rules at any time.
    • 5.8: The moderator has the last say in interpreting the rules.

  • Read all of the rules

Setup
This game will use the Matrix6 setup. This is a semi-open setup.

In this setup, there are 9 players. Three of the roles will be generated randomly by picking a row or column from the matrix. Then one Mafia Goon and 5 Vanilla Townies are added to maintain a 2:7 mafia:town ratio. The six possible setups are:
1. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Jailkeeper, 1 1-Shot Bulletproof Townie, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
2. 6 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Cop, 2 Mafia Goons
3. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Doctor, 1 Town Tracker, 2 Mafia Goons
4. 6 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Jailkeeper, 2 Mafia Goons
5. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Cop, 1 Town Doctor, 1 Mafia Goon, 1 Mafia Roleblocker
6. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 1-Shot Bulletproof Townie, 1 Town Tracker, 2 Mafia Goons


Role PMs
Quote:
You are A Polished Gemstone (Vanilla Townie).

You live in the Box of Treasure and you are proud of being a gemstone and not a mineral. You cannot wait to let the gemstones completely populate the Box of Treasure again, and you believe that minerals do not have a place there.

You have no other power besides your vote and your voice.

You win when all the minerals are eliminated from the Box of Treasure.
Quote:
You are Sapphire (Town Cop).

You love living in the Box of Treasure, so you were devastated to hear that there were possible minerals living among you. Determined to find out who were the imposters, you took the initiative to become the cop of the Box of Treasure. You lurk around at night to see who is a mineral.

Every night, you may target one player to find out their alignment.

You win when all the minerals are eliminated from the Box of Treasure.
Quote:
You are Emerald (Town Doctor).

After hearing the news about possible minerals living amongst you, you quickly realized that it meant some innocent gemstones dying and being kicked out of the Box of Treasure. You decided to take on the role of a doctor, healing gemstones and protecting them from dying. You decide that it is not worth it to kill gemstones who clearly have not done anything wrong.

Every night, you may target someone to prevent them from dying that night. You may not heal yourself two nights in a row.

You win when all the minerals are eliminated from the Box of Treasure.
Quote:
You are Aquamarine (Town Tracker).

You started being suspicious of some of your fellow "gemstones" in the Box of Treasure when the news came out about possible mineral infiltrators. Every night, you decided, you would sneak around and stalk people to see what they do at night. You think this is the best way to sniff out those mineral imposters.

Every night, you may target one player to find out who they visited that night.

You win when all the minerals are eliminated from the Box of Treasure.
Quote:
You are Ruby (Town Jailkeeper).

You were given the job of jailkeeper in the Box of Treasure. You love your job, and you feel that it is your responsibility to help keep the minerals away. You guard a small jail cell in the corner of the Box of Treasure, and you make sure to always do your job as a responsible gemstone.

Every night, you may target one player to jailkeep. Your target will not be able to perform any action that night, and all nightkill attempts against your target that night will be blocked.

You win when all the minerals are eliminated from the Box of Treasure.
Quote:
You are Diamond (1-Shot Bulletproof Townie).

As one of the strongest gemstones that exist, you have a one-time-use protective layer around you that blocks any mineral from trying to throw you out of the Box of Treasures at night. You are proud of being a strong gemstones and sometimes wish that others can be as strong as you.

The first time that a mineral tries to kill you at night, that kill will be negated.

You win when all the minerals are eliminated from the Box of Treasure.
Quote:
You are Amethyst (Mafia Goon).

You once really wanted to join the gemstones in the Box of Treasure, but they rejected you, calling you nothing but "purple quartz". Now that you have snuck into the Box of Treasure, you are ready to get rid of all the gemstones and let the true rulers - minerals, rule.

Every night, you may target one player to eliminate them from the Box of Treasure. You may also talk to your fellow mineral partner here.

You win when all of the gemstones have been eliminated or nothing can prevent this.
Quote:
You are Calcite (Mafia Roleblocker).

You were disappointed when you were rejected from entering the Box of Treasure, but you were okay with it. However, you agreed to help your good mineral friend in bringing the gemstones down when you heard that they were going to sneak into the Box of Treasure. Now you are waiting for your moment for you and you friend to strike.

Every night, you may target one player to roleblock. Your target will not be able to perform any action that night. This action takes precedence over the Jailkeeper. You may also talk to your fellow mineral partner here.

You win when all of the gemstones have been eliminated or nothing can prevent this.


Alive (6)
+pandabear10
+mathjay
MathBeast77
Black-cappedChickadee tigeryong
EntropiaAwake
connorcc


Dead(3)
SpeedCuber7 (Mafia Goon)
+candythecorgi (Vanilla Townie)
AmberTiger79 (Vanilla Townie)


Events
Day 1: SpeedCuber7 hanged.
Night 1: candythecorgi died
Day 2: AmberTiger79 hanged
Night 2: No one died.


PM Prods
 


Warnings
 


Do not post until this line is crossed out

Day 1 begins at 1:00:00 EDT 10 May 2024.

post_rendered:
It was another day in the Box of Treasure. However, something in the air was different. No gemstone could explain it. It just felt different.
Perhaps it was because of the rumor spreading around. It appeared that not all the stones in the Box of Treasure were real gemstones. Some even went to the point where they suggested that there were minerals among them.
Some of the gemstones began to investigate. Some didn't seem to care at all. But their mission was clear now. Eliminate the minerals from the Box of Treasure before anything bad happens.
post_canonical:

Some of the gemstones began to investigate. Some didn't seem to care at all. But their mission was clear now. Eliminate the minerals from the Box of Treasure before anything bad happens.[/color]

post_rendered:
Hello everyone! I'm pandabear10, one of your SEs, which basically means I have some experience in this game. Actually, the SE composition of this game is a bit funny as two of your experienced players (mathjay and myself) started playing a loooooooooooooooooooong time ago, and this (probably) will reflect in our stylistic choices when playing this game.

Let's explain the important rules, shall we?

No editing is a very important rule in this forum. (Well, this thread at least. In non-game threads, editing is chill, and if you're mafia and have a mafia topic then editing there is probably fine.) Anything that goes into this thread stays in this thread. The reason this rule is in place is that in forum mafia, anything that you have said should in principle should be able to be looked up and analyzed at any time. Editing can destroy the past, so we don't allow that. If you made an error in typing or something that you feel should be corrected, just post again stating what was wrong and what it should be. A common acronym for this is "EBWOP" which stands for edit by way of post.

Don't post at night! Day time is for talking and hanging the baddies. Night time is where all the secret stuff happens. Mafia kill someone, any power roles can do whatever, but it must all be uncoordinated (unless you're mafia, in which case you don't talk here but in your thread). Now, it's perfectly fine to coordinate night actions during day time, but if you have a special role and you start to get cold feet, you can't say that when it's night. (Oh, and don't take this as 'it's time to claim right now!'. That's bad for a lot of reasons that I won't get into here.)

Don't post if you're dead (or not in this game). One of the reasons that Mafia might kill someone is that that they feel someone is getting a little too on their case, and so the elimination serves the purpose of controlling the topic. Posting while dead ruins this. Also, dead people sometimes get spoiled by the moderator, in which case revealing that publicly REALLY destroys the point of the game. If you aren't in the game, posting is just cluttering the thread. Please don't.

Don't talk about this game substantively outside of this thread. It's ok to say that you are in this game / you died. That's basically everything that's definitely safe to say out of thread. No speculation outside of this thread (outside communicating directly with fdas or in moderator-approved spaces such as a dead chat) on why you died, who you think is scum, what you would have done differently, etc in spaces other people can see, ESPECIALLY in private to another player. Part of the premise of forum mafia is that all communication between townies is public and not secretly between people. The ability to communicate secretly is actually very powerful when used well (and allowed by the moderator, usually as a form of power role). Don't go prodding people to be active for the moderator. Let fdas do their job, and you do yours.

post_canonical:
Hello everyone! I'm pandabear10, one of your SEs, which basically means I have some experience in this game. Actually, the SE composition of this game is a bit funny as two of your experienced players (mathjay and myself) started playing a loooooooooooooooooooong time ago, and this (probably) will reflect in our stylistic choices when playing this game.

Let's explain the important rules, shall we?

No editing is a very important rule in this forum. (Well, this thread at least. In non-game threads, editing is chill, and if you're mafia and have a mafia topic then editing there is probably fine.) Anything that goes into this thread stays in this thread. The reason this rule is in place is that in forum mafia, anything that you have said should in principle should be able to be looked up and analyzed at any time. Editing can destroy the past, so we don't allow that. If you made an error in typing or something that you feel should be corrected, just post again stating what was wrong and what it should be. A common acronym for this is "EBWOP" which stands for edit by way of post.

Don't post at night! Day time is for talking and hanging the baddies. Night time is where all the secret stuff happens. Mafia kill someone, any power roles can do whatever, but it must all be uncoordinated (unless you're mafia, in which case you don't talk here but in your thread). Now, it's perfectly fine to coordinate night actions during day time, but if you have a special role and you start to get cold feet, you can't say that when it's night. (Oh, and don't take this as 'it's time to claim right now!'. That's bad for a lot of reasons that I won't get into here.)

Don't post if you're dead (or not in this game). One of the reasons that Mafia might kill someone is that that they feel someone is getting a little too on their case, and so the elimination serves the purpose of controlling the topic. Posting while dead ruins this. Also, dead people sometimes get spoiled by the moderator, in which case revealing that publicly REALLY destroys the point of the game. If you aren't in the game, posting is just cluttering the thread. Please don't.

Don't talk about this game substantively outside of this thread. It's ok to say that you are in this game / you died. That's basically everything that's definitely safe to say out of thread.  No speculation outside of this thread (outside communicating directly with fdas or in moderator-approved spaces such as a dead chat) on why you died, who you think is scum, what you would have done differently, etc in spaces other people can see, ESPECIALLY in private to another player. Part of the premise of forum mafia is that all communication between townies is public and not secretly between people. The ability to communicate secretly is actually very powerful when used well (and allowed by the moderator, usually as a form of power role). Don't go prodding people to be active for the moderator. Let fdas do their job, and you do yours.

Then just read the rest of the rules. It's written twice. Get the details right, or ask questions when you need to.

Hello! I'm candythecorgi, another one of your experienced players. I thought to check AoPS for the first time in a week, and between the time I tabbed out and the time I came back on, I got a role PM. Funny how that happened.

Abbreviations and terminology in general can be hard to get a grasp on when you're just starting to play mafia. If you see a word or phrase you don't understand, don't be afraid to ask what it means! I'll also try to remember to explain any that I use in my posts.

Credits to NDMath, I stole this from him and added some others I think are important to know: List of Common Terms
AI - Alignment Indicative - If something is AI, a read can be made off of it.
Bandwagon - To follow the general thinking, or to "sheep" off others (going with the flow)
Breadcrumbing - A post which hides certain clues in a very subtle manner.
CFD - Chinese Fire Drill - Term for a sudden change in elimination target near the end of the day.
Clear - A player that is confirmed to be Town aligned.
Conf/Conftown - see Clear
Confscum - A player that is confirmed to be Anti-Town.
CC - Counter Claim - A role claim that contradicts another role claim; One of the two players must be lying.
CW - Counter Wagon - A wagon formed in opposition of the leading wagon.
EBWOP - Edit by way of Post - Making a new post to correct something because you are not allowed to edit posts in Mafia.
Elimination - Each day one player can be voted out and is therefore eliminated.
EOD - End of Day
Flavor - The story for a game, and role pm text not related to the role's functions.
Fluff - Content produced by a player that is not related to the game.
FoS - Finger of Suspicion
Hammer - The last vote on a certain person before they are eliminated.
Hang - Voted out of the game, synonym to eliminated.
IC - Inexperience Challenged, basically a very experienced in the community to help newbies learn the game.
ISO - All of a player's posts in a game make up their ISO for that game. (think isolation of their posts)
Lurking - When a certain person barely contributes to discussion, which could be considered scummy.
LYLO - ELiminate or Lose, state where you must keep eliminating mafia to win (one misstep = lose)
L-n - For a natural number , this means a person needs more votes in order to be eliminated.
MYLO - Miseliminate and Lose, state where you must keep eliminating mafia OR vote No Elimination to win (eliminate town = lose)
Newb Card - When a new player uses the excuse of simply being a newbie when a mistake is made.
NK - Abbreviation for Night Kill.
Null - Referring to a read that is either undecided or neutral.
OMGUS - OMG You Suck, I'm gonna vote you because you voted me. (Used in joking fashion or to describe a scummy vote or read)
OP - original post; In a mafia game this is always the post with all the rules and such.
Peek - Term for an investigative role's (such as Cop or Tracker) nightly action result.
POE - Process of Elimination - Removing players believed to be town to leave a smaller "POE" of players that could be mafia.
Power Roles (PR) - Roles with additional power (In this game Cop, Doctor, Tracker, and Neighborizer are the possible Town Power Roles)
Quickhammer - A hammering vote that occurs either suddenly or well before the deadline.
Reads - The main methods for town progress in mafia games. A player can townread or scumread another player based off of their posts, meaning that they think that player is likely to be of that alignment.
Roleclaim (RC) - When someone asks another to claim what their role is.
RQS - Random Question Stage - List of questions during RVS usually containing nickname and pronouns.
RVS - Random Voting Stage - Period at the start of most mafia games of making votes for somewhat random reasons to promote discussion.
Scum/scummy - Synonymous to "mafia"/ likely to be mafia
SOD - Start of Day
Softing - When someone implies what their role is without specifically claiming it.
SE - Semi-Experienced, basically an experienced in the community to help newbies learn the game.
TWTBW - "Too wolfy to be wolf," usually used to express the read that the player in question if wolfy, but that they would care more about how wolfy they look if they were actually a wolf.
Towncore - A group of players you strongly believe are town.
V/LA - Vacation/Limited Access.
Vanity Wagon - Usually referring to just one or two votes as a wagon, implying that there is not enough support for the target player to be eliminated that day.
Wagon - A group of votes on the same player at the same time.
Wall/Wallpost - A long post.


My answers to panda's RQs (random questions):
1. candy
2. any pronouns
3. I joined Mafia Universe in December, and that is where I primarily play now.
4. Games 225 (newbie), 228, 229, 230, 240, 241, 243, 244, 247, 250, 259, 272, 274, 275, 276, 277, 278 (player abandoned), 279, 281, 283, and 285

I wrote this a while back, when I didn't realize I should always capitalize and space these things. I'll edit it if that would be helpful.
candythecorgi wrote:
There are five main ways to find scum:

tone, blending, and motivation
- when they enter the thread, do they seem disconnected, like they have trouble getting into the game and don't really have anyone to rely on? this doesn't always happen, of course, but it's usually indicative of town when it does.
- when does this person lose motivation? for example, if A was pushing for B to be hanged and B flipped town, A would likely feel demotivated as town. However, if A was scum, this wouldn't really matter to them and instead be another step towards a win.
- are they contributing despite being demotivated, or do they use their demotivation as an excuse to not do anything? the first is indicative of town, and the second of scum.
- in general, how many genuine thoughts are they contributing? if all their reads are aggressively uncontroversial, or if they're not making many reads and all their posts are fluff, they're more likely to be scum.
- do they sound like they're extremely self aware and overcompensating to look more towny? they're more likely to be scum (unless there's meta to contest this).

threadstate
- if a wagon forms on someone quickly and without much pushback, they're likely to be town since if they were scum, their partners would defend them.
- if multiple people agree that someone is scummy, but they're not really being voted or pushed, they're more likely to be scum since their partners would be preventing the wagon from getting traction.

night kill analysis
The night kill is the mafia's chance to shut down anyone who might be a threat to them. If the person who is killed isn't consensus town or the most active poster in the game, it should immediately raise red flags as to why they were killed.

Say that A is someone like this. If A scumread B and C, and A was killed in the night, B and C would be high priority suspects. After realizing this and pointing it out to the thread, you can then use the other methods to decide whether B or C is scum, or if both of them are.

agenda
- are they townreading or scumreading someone for no good reason? does their level of confidence feel appropriate?
- on the opposite end of the spectrum, is there someone they're refusing to give a solid opinion on? these two people might be partnered, or the first might be gauging if they can pocket the second. if A didn't have a solid opinion on B before, but scumreads B as soon as B scumreads them, A is more likely to be scum and B town. same thing if B townreads A and A immediately townreads them back. but do make sure to check if A has valid reasons for thinking B's read on A is bad.
- are all their top scumreads people who are relatively new or aren't posting much? if so, they might be scum trying to get an easy hang on town. this doesn't always apply, so evaluate it on a situational basis.
- are they curious about what other people think (towny), or do they let things that should be interesting or suspicious to them go ignored (scummy)?
- does it look like they're genuinely trying to figure out people's alignments (towny), or are they locked into their solve and unwilling to reconsider (scummy)?

agreeing or disagreeing with others
This point is very simple. Read the posts that other people make. If they have a point, feel free to say they convinced you. If you don't agree with their reasoning, express that too and say why. If you don't agree and their reasoning seems fake, that ties back to the agenda point and they might be scum.


You don't have to constantly be thinking about all of this. Feel free to focus on just a few things to read people on, and of course you can check back to this message if you need to :>

As for not dying, it's directly tied to finding scum. If people see that you make an effort to scumhunt and are having genuine thoughts, they're much less likely to hang you.

I also left out interactions, but those are a bit more complicated so it's fine.

post_rendered:
If you're mafia... good luck. May thy knife chip and shatter.

You need to hang townies and avoid getting hanged yourself.

In thread (this game thread, where you will interact with town as well as your mafia partner), you can do this by 1) looking like town, and 2) pushing for townies to get hanged. You will need to come up with believable reasons for the reads that you have. Have a goal in mind for most of the posts that you make. Does claiming that I'm town make it look like I'm eager to play the game as a townie? Will this read help push out a townie? Can I delay my partner's death if I defend them? Etc.

Your night actions are an important asset. At night, you can eliminate people who think you're wolf and might get you hanged. You can also get rid of town PRs (power roles), who make your job much harder. During the first in-game day, PRs will usually lie low as to not get killed by you. They might be more apathetic towards scumreads on them, since they know they can get out of it by claiming their role. Starting from the second day, you can keep looking for that as well as people who insist on reads without having strong reasons for them- this might indicate that they have mechanical information, such as a cop check. If one of you is a Mafia Roleblocker, target the people you think are PRs.

Be confident. Go in with the mindset that you're going to win. Talk to your mafia partner- make plans and build rapport. It's much easier to win that way.

Uh. People don't usually give advice on how to play as wolf, but I think it's allowed? Lolme if I break the rules in my first SE game. Fdas can't replace me

candythecorgi wrote:
Hello! I'm candythecorgi, another one of your experienced players. I thought to check AoPS for the first time in a week, and between the time I tabbed out and the time I came back on, I got a role PM. Funny how that happened.

Abbreviations and terminology in general can be hard to get a grasp on when you're just starting to play mafia. If you see a word or phrase you don't understand, don't be afraid to ask what it means! I'll also try to remember to explain any that I use in my posts.

Credits to NDMath, I stole this from him and added some others I think are important to know: List of Common Terms
AI - Alignment Indicative - If something is AI, a read can be made off of it.
Bandwagon - To follow the general thinking, or to "sheep" off others (going with the flow)
Breadcrumbing - A post which hides certain clues in a very subtle manner.
CFD - Chinese Fire Drill - Term for a sudden change in elimination target near the end of the day.
Clear - A player that is confirmed to be Town aligned.
Conf/Conftown - see Clear
Confscum - A player that is confirmed to be Anti-Town.
CC - Counter Claim - A role claim that contradicts another role claim; One of the two players must be lying.
CW - Counter Wagon - A wagon formed in opposition of the leading wagon.
EBWOP - Edit by way of Post - Making a new post to correct something because you are not allowed to edit posts in Mafia.
Elimination - Each day one player can be voted out and is therefore eliminated.
EOD - End of Day
Flavor - The story for a game, and role pm text not related to the role's functions.
Fluff - Content produced by a player that is not related to the game.
FoS - Finger of Suspicion
Hammer - The last vote on a certain person before they are eliminated.
Hang - Voted out of the game, synonym to eliminated.
IC - Inexperience Challenged, basically a very experienced in the community to help newbies learn the game.
ISO - All of a player's posts in a game make up their ISO for that game. (think isolation of their posts)
Lurking - When a certain person barely contributes to discussion, which could be considered scummy.
LYLO - ELiminate or Lose, state where you must keep eliminating mafia to win (one misstep = lose)
L-n - For a natural number , this means a person needs more votes in order to be eliminated.
MYLO - Miseliminate and Lose, state where you must keep eliminating mafia OR vote No Elimination to win (eliminate town = lose)
Newb Card - When a new player uses the excuse of simply being a newbie when a mistake is made.
NK - Abbreviation for Night Kill.
Null - Referring to a read that is either undecided or neutral.
OMGUS - OMG You Suck, I'm gonna vote you because you voted me. (Used in joking fashion or to describe a scummy vote or read)
OP - original post; In a mafia game this is always the post with all the rules and such.
Peek - Term for an investigative role's (such as Cop or Tracker) nightly action result.
POE - Process of Elimination - Removing players believed to be town to leave a smaller "POE" of players that could be mafia.
Power Roles (PR) - Roles with additional power (In this game Cop, Doctor, Tracker, and Neighborizer are the possible Town Power Roles)
Quickhammer - A hammering vote that occurs either suddenly or well before the deadline.
Reads - The main methods for town progress in mafia games. A player can townread or scumread another player based off of their posts, meaning that they think that player is likely to be of that alignment.
Roleclaim (RC) - When someone asks another to claim what their role is.
RQS - Random Question Stage - List of questions during RVS usually containing nickname and pronouns.
RVS - Random Voting Stage - Period at the start of most mafia games of making votes for somewhat random reasons to promote discussion.
Scum/scummy - Synonymous to "mafia"/ likely to be mafia
SOD - Start of Day
Softing - When someone implies what their role is without specifically claiming it.
SE - Semi-Experienced, basically an experienced in the community to help newbies learn the game.
TWTBW - "Too wolfy to be wolf," usually used to express the read that the player in question if wolfy, but that they would care more about how wolfy they look if they were actually a wolf.
Towncore - A group of players you strongly believe are town.
V/LA - Vacation/Limited Access.
Vanity Wagon - Usually referring to just one or two votes as a wagon, implying that there is not enough support for the target player to be eliminated that day.
Wagon - A group of votes on the same player at the same time.
Wall/Wallpost - A long post.


My answers to panda's RQs (random questions):
1. candy
2. any pronouns
3. I joined Mafia Universe in December, and that is where I primarily play now.
4. Games 225 (newbie), 228, 229, 230, 240, 241, 243, 244, 247, 250, 259, 272, 274, 275, 276, 277, 278 (player abandoned), 279, 281, 283, and 285

candythecorgi wrote:
If you're mafia... good luck. May thy knife chip and shatter.

You need to hang townies and avoid getting hanged yourself.

In thread (this game thread, where you will interact with town as well as your mafia partner), you can do this by 1) looking like town, and 2) pushing for townies to get hanged. You will need to come up with believable reasons for the reads that you have. Have a goal in mind for most of the posts that you make. Does claiming that I'm town make it look like I'm eager to play the game as a townie? Will this read help push out a townie? Can I delay my partner's death if I defend them? Etc.

Your night actions are an important asset. At night, you can eliminate people who think you're wolf and might get you hanged. You can also get rid of town PRs (power roles), who make your job much harder. During the first in-game day, PRs will usually lie low as to not get killed by you. They might be more apathetic towards scumreads on them, since they know they can get out of it by claiming their role. Starting from the second day, you can keep looking for that as well as people who insist on reads without having strong reasons for them- this might indicate that they have mechanical information, such as a cop check. If one of you is a Mafia Roleblocker, target the people you think are PRs.

Be confident. Go in with the mindset that you're going to win. Talk to your mafia partner- make plans and build rapport. It's much easier to win that way.

AmberTiger79 wrote:
why are there so many wall posts?

A playstyle thing, although I think candy and I are responsible for the big ones. When I come back to Mafia after a few hours of being away, barring something urgent I tend to collect all of my thoughts into a single post rather than making several small posts.

EntropiaAwake wrote:
Sorry, I’ve been a bit inactive, I see everyone is giving their opinions so I guess I’ll give mine. From reading this conversation it’s a bit weird how candy hasn't gave a reason why he is suspicious of speed cuber but he could just be inactive.

Alright I'm going to explain a few things here. candy AFAIK does have a meta of lurking/inactivity when scum - referencing MG285 here. HOWEVER, I do not see that as a direct reason why I would read candy as scum. This is actually a point that I think puts me at odds with some of the other people in this forum. In my opinion, the proper way to use player meta is to establish that behavior that would ordinarily be towny/scummy for a particular player is actually NAI (not an indicator) for that player due to past games where they were scum/town while exhibiting that same behavior. However, I believe that using meta to establish that a player is "in their town meta" or "in their scum meta" tends to be unreliable at best, and at worst (and I believe it usually is at worst) complete WIFOM (circular argument), especially with more advanced players who are able to fake their own meta. In other words, I only use meta to invalidate evidence rather than provide new evidence. Thus, although one might argue that candy is "in their scum meta" by being inactive, I don't see this as a scummy thing for candy in particular. Now, don't get me wrong, being inactive generally speaking is slightly scummy behavior. But due to the current threadstate and LITERALLY EVERYONE being inactive, scumreading someone for being inactive in general also feels a little disingenuous.

That said, someone 'being in their scum meta' might prompt me to examine their posts more closely, but I would not cite it as a reason to town/scumread someone.

ty for listening to my tedtalk

Ironically I’m a bit suspicious of speed cuber because he is being a bit “loud” I guess, I can’t think of a better way to put it, though that may just be his personally and he is town.

This can actually be valid; you could be believing that speedcuber is attempting to "powerwolf", that is, take over discussion as scum. It can be hard to determine what is actually legitimate attempts to differentiate genuine attempts to help from powerwolfing, but maybe one characteristic I can try to give is that powerwolfing is more likely to be extremely insistent (due to scum's hidden agenda). Experienced players powerwolfing know how to avoid this though, but can still sometimes be seen. Do you think speedcuber fits this definition? (I actually do have an opinion on this but I want to hear Entropia before I say anything.)

EntropiaAwake wrote:
whats pocketing?

Pocketing is... hmm, it's when you make someone believe that you are town more than they logically should. This can come in the form of interacting with them more than usual, saying that you like them as a person, townreading them, agreeing with them, or doing something you know they would townread you for.

Most of the time it means the person trying to pocket someone else is mafia, but townies joke about pocketing other people, and the definition has also expanded to be a description of general behavior rather than just scummy behavior.

If you see someone say something like "mathjay pocketed me" without following it up with a scumread on mathjay, it's probably a lighthearted way of expressing that they townread mathjay, but that it might not be a well founded read.

In this case, I am afraid that you, as mafia, pocketed me by asking for my input. But to be clear it's not a serious suspicion- paranoia only means that I don't entirely trust my townread of you.

Story
The gemstones talked amongst each other.
"Who could it be?" one asked.
"I don't know, why are you asking me?" another replied. The discussion lasted for an entire day, until a conclusion was made.
"Does everyone agree on this stone?" someone from the crowd yelled. Everyone murmured their agreement. "GOODBYE!" the stone from the crowd yelled.

There was a blast of what seemed like a mixture of fire and something else. When everything calmed down, all there was left was a pile of shattered stone.

SpeedCuber7 was hanged. They were Amethyst (Mafia Goon).

post_rendered:
Everyone looked around, trying to see if anything changed. If anyone was gone. And indeed, one of them was missing.
All the stones gathered around together for the great reveal. “Who’s missing?” they murmured amongst each other. They were all anxious to see if the missing stone was a mineral.
Finally, a gemstone walked up to the crowd. It stepped aside to reveal a shattered stone. Everyone leaned forward to see if they could recognize the stone from the pieces on the ground. Yet they couldn’t. They soon understood why.
“It’s just a regular gemstone, except that it’s polished!” said the gemstone that was revealing the shattered stone.
Sighs of disappointment went up the crowd. It was another of them gone. And the last mineral was still not dead.

candythecorgi has died. They were a polished gemstone (vanilla townie).

Day 2 has started.

post_rendered:
Ok, either I’m blind dumb or both. I cannot find the meaning of that anywhere in the slang list

Click to reveal hidden text
AI - Alignment Indicative - If something is AI, a read can be made off of it.
Bandwagon - To follow the general thinking, or to "sheep" off others (going with the flow)
Breadcrumbing - A post which hides certain clues in a very subtle manner.
CFD - Chinese Fire Drill - Term for a sudden change in elimination target near the end of the day.
Clear - A player that is confirmed to be Town aligned.
Conf/Conftown - see Clear
Confscum - A player that is confirmed to be Anti-Town.
CC - Counter Claim - A role claim that contradicts another role claim; One of the two players must be lying.
CW - Counter Wagon - A wagon formed in opposition of the leading wagon.
EBWOP - Edit by way of Post - Making a new post to correct something because you are not allowed to edit posts in Mafia.
Elimination - Each day one player can be voted out and is therefore eliminated.
EOD - End of Day
Flavor - The story for a game, and role pm text not related to the role's functions.
Fluff - Content produced by a player that is not related to the game.
FoS - Finger of Suspicion
Hammer - The last vote on a certain person before they are eliminated.
Hang - Voted out of the game, synonym to eliminated.
IC - Inexperience Challenged, basically a very experienced in the community to help newbies learn the game.
ISO - All of a player's posts in a game make up their ISO for that game. (think isolation of their posts)
Lurking - When a certain person barely contributes to discussion, which could be considered scummy.
LYLO - ELiminate or Lose, state where you must keep eliminating mafia to win (one misstep = lose)
L-n - For a natural number , this means a person needs more votes in order to be eliminated.
MYLO - Miseliminate and Lose, state where you must keep eliminating mafia OR vote No Elimination to win (eliminate town = lose)
Newb Card - When a new player uses the excuse of simply being a newbie when a mistake is made.
NK - Abbreviation for Night Kill.
Null - Referring to a read that is either undecided or neutral.
OMGUS - OMG You Suck, I'm gonna vote you because you voted me. (Used in joking fashion or to describe a scummy vote or read)
OP - original post; In a mafia game this is always the post with all the rules and such.
Peek - Term for an investigative role's (such as Cop or Tracker) nightly action result.
POE - Process of Elimination - Removing players believed to be town to leave a smaller "POE" of players that could be mafia.
Power Roles (PR) - Roles with additional power (In this game Cop, Doctor, Tracker, and Neighborizer are the possible Town Power Roles)
Quickhammer - A hammering vote that occurs either suddenly or well before the deadline.
Reads - The main methods for town progress in mafia games. A player can townread or scumread another player based off of their posts, meaning that they think that player is likely to be of that alignment.
Roleclaim (RC) - When someone asks another to claim what their role is.
RQS - Random Question Stage - List of questions during RVS usually containing nickname and pronouns.
RVS - Random Voting Stage - Period at the start of most mafia games of making votes for somewhat random reasons to promote discussion.
Scum/scummy - Synonymous to "mafia"/ likely to be mafia
SOD - Start of Day
Softing - When someone implies what their role is without specifically claiming it.
SE - Semi-Experienced, basically an experienced in the community to help newbies learn the game.
TWTBW - "Too wolfy to be wolf," usually used to express the read that the player in question if wolfy, but that they would care more about how wolfy they look if they were actually a wolf.
Towncore - A group of players you strongly believe are town.
V/LA - Vacation/Limited Access.
Vanity Wagon - Usually referring to just one or two votes as a wagon, implying that there is not enough support for the target player to be eliminated that day.
Wagon - A group of votes on the same player at the same time.
Wall/Wallpost - A long post.

Hammer - The last vote on a certain person before they are eliminated.
Hang - Voted out of the game, synonym to eliminated.
IC - Inexperience Challenged, basically a very experienced in the community to help newbies learn the game.
ISO - All of a player's posts in a game make up their ISO for that game. (think isolation of their posts)
Lurking - When a certain person barely contributes to discussion, which could be considered scummy.
LYLO - ELiminate or Lose, state where you must keep eliminating mafia to win (one misstep = lose)
L-n - For a natural number $n$, this means a person needs $n$ more votes in order to be eliminated.
MYLO - Miseliminate and Lose, state where you must keep eliminating mafia OR vote No Elimination to win (eliminate town = lose)
Newb Card - When a new player uses the excuse of simply being a newbie when a mistake is made.
NK - Abbreviation for Night Kill.
Null - Referring to a read that is either undecided or neutral.
OMGUS - OMG You Suck, I'm gonna vote you because you voted me. (Used in joking fashion or to describe a scummy vote or read)
OP - original post; In a mafia game this is always the post with all the rules and such.
Peek - Term for an investigative role's (such as Cop or Tracker) nightly action result.
POE - Process of Elimination - Removing players believed to be town to leave a smaller "POE" of players that could be mafia.
Power Roles (PR) - Roles with additional power (In this game Cop, Doctor, Tracker, and Neighborizer are the possible Town Power Roles)
Quickhammer - A hammering vote that occurs either suddenly or well before the deadline.
Reads - The main methods for town progress in mafia games. A player can townread or scumread another player based off of their posts, meaning that they think that player is likely to be of that alignment.
Roleclaim (RC) - When someone asks another to claim what their role is.
RQS - Random Question Stage - List of questions during RVS usually containing nickname and pronouns.
RVS - Random Voting Stage - Period at the start of most mafia games of making votes for somewhat random reasons to promote discussion.
Scum/scummy - Synonymous to "mafia"/ likely to be mafia
SOD - Start of Day
Softing - When someone implies what their role is without specifically claiming it.
SE - Semi-Experienced, basically an experienced in the community to help newbies learn the game.
TWTBW - "Too wolfy to be wolf," usually used to express the read that the player in question if wolfy, but that they would care more about how wolfy they look if they were actually a wolf.
Towncore - A group of players you strongly believe are town.
V/LA - Vacation/Limited Access.
Vanity Wagon - Usually referring to just one or two votes as a wagon, implying that there is not enough support for the target player to be eliminated that day.
Wagon - A group of votes on the same player at the same time.
Wall/Wallpost - A long post.[/hide]

post_rendered:
Commonly used Mafia Terms (from MU)
Created by EulerNTM
#
#-Shot - When a role can only be used a certain amount of times during a game, with # representing that number.

3P - Third Party - Players that are not aligned with the town or mafia.

A
AI - Alignment Indicative - an action or post by a player that IS indicative of whether they are aligned with the town or mafia

Angleshooting - Angleshooting or to "angleshoot" is violating the spirit of the rules without actually breaking the rules. Angleshooting is not technically cheating, but is considered equally unacceptable. Explained in depth on the forum rules post.

Anti-spew - Usually used to refer to when a mafia-aligned player is very close to being killed and goes into “anti-spew”, where they are careful about their wording so that town are not able to determine who is/isn’t their partner(s) after they flip mafia.

AtE - Appeal to Emotion - When a player makes a post in order to evoke sympathy so that they can avoid being hanged.

B
Bandwagon - When player(s) hop onto a vote wagon.

BM - Short for "Bastard Mod", who can lie to players about game mechanics (including in their role PM), uses a relatively screwed setup where players might change roles, alliances, and sometimes with messed up, new roles. Generally not good for a newbie game.

BP - Bulletproof - When a player is immune to attack(s) during the night phase or during ITAs, either because they have a bulletproof modifier or a bulletproof vest.

BPV - Bulletproof Vest - A vest which is bulletproof, usually only takes one shot.

Breadcrumbing - When a player leaves clues as to what their role is/what their night actions are, in case they die without the information being revealed.

Bussing - when a mafia-aligned player votes or pushes a fellow mafia-aligned player in order to appear as if they are town-aligned whenever the mafia-aligned player they are voting has their role revealed.

C
CC - Counterclaim - when a player claims the same role as another player.

Cemetery - a place where dead players can talk about the game freely. Also known as the graveyard, DVC, or Dead Villager Chat.

CFD - Chinese Fire Drill - When a group of players suddenly switch their vote somewhere else at last minute, usually another player who wasn’t seen as an option for the hang at that point in the phase.

Claim Fishing - When a player tries to get another player to reveal their role, usually done in a subtle fashion.

Crossvoting - when two people are voting on each other. Usually in reference to MYLO or LYLO states where if Person A is voted by Person B, then either the game is over if they're both town (as mafia-aligned players can vote over Player B with majority) or there's confirmed a mafia-aligned player between the two of them.

Counterwagon - the person with the second highest amount of votes other than the person who was hanged, or in larger or more intricate games a person with enough votes on them to be considered in contention for the hang.

D
D1/D2… etc. - Day phase

DADV - Dead Air, Dead Villager - When communication in the thread has gone down, so players infer that it’s because the hang is a villager.

Derpclear - When someone seemingly shows a lack of knowledge about an open/semi-open setup, or any other information that a mafia-aligned player would be expected to know, and is then cleared for this reason by another player.

Distancing - When two mafia-aligned players try to make it seem like they are not aligned with each other.

DVC - Dead Villager Chat, otherwise known as the graveyard or cemetery. Despite the name, dead wolves are generally allowed to participate.

E
EOD - End of Day

F
F3/F5/F# - A point in the game at which # of players are alive. Generally, but not always, either used to describe a LYLO or MYLO situation or one where there is otherwise significantly less players alive than there were at game start.

Fakeclaiming - When a player claims a role that they do not have.

Flavor - The game’s theme, sometimes what roles are based off of, and creates a story usually based on what happens in the game.

Fluffposting/Fluffing - When a player posts nothing of content.

FPS - Fancy Play Syndrome - When a player uses an elaborate strategy over a more simple one.

Frozen - When a player has stopped posting, often after being called out as mafia in the thread.
Alternative: Thread is frozen and no one is posting.

G
Gambit - Any play which is made in order to evoke a certain reaction, which can be made from both alignments.

Gamethrowing - When a player actively makes an action to intentionally throw the game for their alignment; examples of this can include mafia hardclaiming mafia without a strategic reason and/or revealing who the mafia team is in the thread.

GOAT - Greatest of All Time

H
Hammer - A vote that’s placed on a player which causes the wagon to reach majority in order to hang the player that the wagon is on, ending the day phase after the vote is placed.

Hard Claim - to claim a role with there being no uncertain terms - see Soft Claim, is relevant.

Hedging - When a player does not commit to a stance and stays on the fence regarding a topic, usually the alignment of a player.

Hydra - An account shared by two or more players that share a role and have their own private chat.

I
ISO - Isolating a specific player’s posts in order to look at all of them in one place.

ITA - In Thread Attack - A mechanic used in most mashes where every player has a X% hit rate shot they may use in the thread during designated times to attempt to kill someone.

ITT - In The Thread

J
JOAT - Jack Of All Trades - A role where there are multiple (usually 1-use actions) that the player can use. Can be either mafia-aligned or town-aligned.

K
KP - Kill Power - A role of either town or mafia alignment that has the power to kill another player at night.

L
LHF - Low Hanging Fruit - A player relatively easy to get hang as either alignment.

Lolcatting - When a mafia-aligned player is confirmed mafia, often mechanically through night actions, and starts posting random stuff.

LYLO - Hang or Lose - A game state where the town must hang a non-town-aligned player or they lose. This is because the town has majority by only one player.

M
Meta - Meta overall refers to the shared history and knowledge of each other's playing styles that comes from having played with each other across several games. A meta read is when someone relies on their knowledge of another player's past behavior in a certain alignment in order to form an informed read.

Modkill - When a player is killed by the host or moderator for rule breaking during a game. This can include breaking the rules on OGI, angleshooting, or behavioral issues.

Multi - A secondary account of someone else. Any multi's found on AoPS are permabanned.

Multi-ISO - Isolating the posts of multiple people so that only the posts of the selected people are outputted.

MYLO - Mishang and Lose - A gamestate where the town must hang a mafia-aligned player or vote for no hang in order not to lose. Town has majority by two players in this gamestate.

N
N1/N2… etc. - Night phase

NAI - Not Alignment Indicative - an action or post by a player which is not indicative of whether they are aligned with the town or mafia.

NK - Night Kill - The player(s) who were killed that night, either by mafia-aligned players or town-aligned players with killing power.

NL - No Hang - When no player is hang at the end of the day phase. A common strategy used during MYLO to force the mafia to kill another town member, narrowing down the pool of suspects.

O
Openwolving - When a player talks in a way that obviously indicates mafia behaviour.

OMGUS - Oh My Gosh You Suck - When a player votes someone because the other person voted them.

P
Peek - When a town investigative uses their night action on another player, and gets information on their alignment at the end of the night phase.

Pocketing - when a player tries to gain the townread of another player, usually made by mafia attempting to pocket town

POE - Process of Elimination - A strategy which attempts to identify players who aren’t cleared, and hang amongst those uncleared players in order to find mafia-aligned players. A list of who one wishes to hang are also often referred to as PoE.

PR - Power Role - A role that is not vanilla, and has special abilities in the setup. Can refer to both mafia-aligned and town-aligned players.

Q
N/A

R
Roleclaim - To claim your role in the thread, and is used as a tactic to avoid a hang or reveal that you are a PR.

RVS - Random Voting Stage - Occurs at the start of the first day phase, and is used to get the game started by randomly voting until solid evidence builds up. Often lasts until the middle of the first day phase, sometimes ends sooner.

RQS - Random Questioning Stage - Occurs at the start of the first day phase, and is used to get general information about players, like gender or past games. Often ends after all players have answered the questions, sometimes ends sooner.

RWSO - Rand Wolf, Sub Out - A belief that a player who rands mafia, or a specific player in general, is more likely to sub out after being randed in a mafia-aligned slot, when compared to being randed as a town aligned slot. Note that subbing out in a way that is impacted by alignment is against site rules, and so is speculation on why someone subbed out; people generally use this in a non-serious manner, and you shouldn’t be using it in a game.

S
Scum - Another word for mafia-aligned players

Scumbaiting - When a town-aligned player purposely acts like a PR in order to attract the night kill.

Scumhunting - When a player tries to deduce which players are mafia-aligned.

SOD - Start of Day

Soft Claim - to imply that you are a role without directly stating it in the thread. Also can be considered breadcrumbing - see Hard Claim, semi-relevant.

Spewing - Used to refer to when a flipped mafia-aligned player has given away information about who is or isn’t a member of the mafia (or when they're doing it while still alive).

T
Thunderdome - Two players attempting to get each other hanged with at least one attempting to force the game to hang between the two until one of them flips mafia, often a term used in LYLO where two players have already crossvoted; can also be shortened to "tdome".

Tinfoil - A theory or thought that relies on something highly unlikely being the case, often entertained or brought up out of paranoia.

TMI - Too Much Information - This is used as reasoning for someone being a wolf. It revolves around the idea that the post(s) the person is making are too well-informed, so they must be mafia since the mafia already know who the rest of the mafia are.

Tunnel - When a player is focusing on one other player, accusing only them of being scum and generally not trying to solve the rest of the game.

TWTBAW - Too Wolfy To Be A Wolf - This concept is where one person appears to be so suspicious that they seem too suspicious to be mafia-aligned, hence being Too Wolfy To Be A Wolf. This is often attributed to the fact that they would be more self-conscious about being so wolfy, and would be attempting to appear more like a town member if they were actually mafia-aligned.

U
N/A

V
VCA - Vote Count Analysis - Vote count analysis is a common strategy using previous votecounts to try to find town or scum, typically looking at player composition of wagons on certain players and what those players flipped, or if they have changed their vote to save certain players.

VT - Vanilla Townie - A town role with no special abilities.

Vanilla - A role with no special abilities (both Mafia Goon and Vanilla Townie fall under this category)

Villager - Another word for town-aligned player.

W
Wagon - Refers to the number of votes a player has that makes them a candidate for the hang.

WIFOM - Wine In Front Of Me - Term originating from this scene in The Princess Bride movie. Used to denote when someone is trying to guess or reason what choice a person made, when that person knew in advance that his/her choices would be subject to scrutiny.

WIL - Wants It Less - Refers to when players are showing a lack of desire to win. Often jokingly or mockingly used to denote players who played poorly.

WIM - Wants It More - Refers to the attribute of extreme passion/desire to win that some players possess.

WOAT - Worst of All Time

Wolf - Another word for mafia-aligned players.

X
N/A

Y
N/A

Z
N/A
post_canonical:
[hide=Commonly used Mafia Terms (from MU)]
Created by EulerNTM
[hide=#]

[b]#-Shot[/b] - When a role can only be used a certain amount of times during a game, with # representing that number.

[b]3P[/b] - [b]Third Party[/b] - Players that are not aligned with the town or mafia.[/hide]
[hide=A]

[b]AI[/b] - [b]Alignment Indicative[/b] - an action or post by a player that IS indicative of whether they are aligned with the town or mafia

[b]Angleshooting[/b] - Angleshooting or to "angleshoot" is violating the spirit of the rules without actually breaking the rules. Angleshooting is not technically cheating, but is considered equally unacceptable. Explained in depth on the forum rules post.

[b]Anti-spew[/b] - Usually used to refer to when a mafia-aligned player is very close to being killed and goes into “anti-spew”, where they are careful about their wording so that town are not able to determine who is/isn’t their partner(s) after they flip mafia.

[b]AtE[/b] - [b]Appeal to Emotion[/b] - When a player makes a post in order to evoke sympathy so that they can avoid being hanged.[/hide]
[hide=B]

[b]Bandwagon[/b] - When player(s) hop onto a vote wagon.

[b]BM[/b] - Short for "Bastard Mod", who can lie to players about game mechanics (including in their role PM), uses a relatively screwed setup where players might change roles, alliances, and sometimes with messed up, new roles. Generally not good for a newbie game.

[b]BP[/b] - [b]Bulletproof[/b] - When a player is immune to attack(s) during the night phase or during ITAs, either because they have a bulletproof modifier or a bulletproof vest.

[b]BPV[/b] - [b]Bulletproof Vest[/b] - A vest which is bulletproof, usually only takes one shot.

[b]Breadcrumbing[/b] - When a player leaves clues as to what their role is/what their night actions are, in case they die without the information being revealed.

[b]Bussing[/b] - when a mafia-aligned player votes or pushes a fellow mafia-aligned player in order to appear as if they are town-aligned whenever the mafia-aligned player they are voting has their role revealed.[/hide]
[hide=C]
[b]CC[/b] - [b]Counterclaim[/b] - when a player claims the same role as another player.

[b]Cemetery[/b] - a place where dead players can talk about the game freely. Also known as the graveyard, DVC, or Dead Villager Chat.

[b]CFD[/b] - [b]Chinese Fire Drill[/b] - When a group of players suddenly switch their vote somewhere else at last minute, usually another player who wasn’t seen as an option for the hang at that point in the phase.

[b]Claim Fishing[/b] - When a player tries to get another player to reveal their role, usually done in a subtle fashion.

[b]Crossvoting[/b] - when two people are voting on each other. Usually in reference to MYLO or LYLO states where if Person A is voted by Person B, then either the game is over if they're both town (as mafia-aligned players can vote over Player B with majority) or there's confirmed a mafia-aligned player between the two of them.

[b]Counterwagon[/b] - the person with the second highest amount of votes other than the person who was hanged, or in larger or more intricate games a person with enough votes on them to be considered in contention for the hang.
[/hide]
[hide=D]

[b]D1/D2… etc.[/b] - Day phase

[b]DADV[/b] - [b]Dead Air, Dead Villager[/b] - When communication in the thread has gone down, so players infer that it’s because the hang is a villager.

[b]Derpclear[/b] - When someone seemingly shows a lack of knowledge about an open/semi-open setup, or any other information that a mafia-aligned player would be expected to know, and is then cleared for this reason by another player.

[b]Distancing[/b] - When two mafia-aligned players try to make it seem like they are not aligned with each other.

[b]DVC[/b] - Dead Villager Chat, otherwise known as the graveyard or cemetery. Despite the name, dead wolves are generally allowed to participate.[/hide]
[hide=E]

[b]EOD[/b] - End of Day[/hide]
[hide=F]

[b]F3/F5/F#[/b] - A point in the game at which # of players are alive. Generally, but not always, either used to describe a LYLO or MYLO situation or one where there is otherwise significantly less players alive than there were at game start.

[b]Fakeclaiming[/b] - When a player claims a role that they do not have.

[b]Flavor[/b] - The game’s theme, sometimes what roles are based off of, and creates a story usually based on what happens in the game.

[b]Fluffposting/Fluffing[/b] - When a player posts nothing of content.

[b]FPS[/b] - [b]Fancy Play Syndrome[/b] - When a player uses an elaborate strategy over a more simple one.

[b]Frozen[/b] - When a player has stopped posting, often after being called out as mafia in the thread.
Alternative: Thread is frozen and no one is posting.
[/hide]
[hide=G]

[b]Gambit[/b] - Any play which is made in order to evoke a certain reaction, which can be made from both alignments.

[b]Gamethrowing[/b] - When a player actively makes an action to intentionally throw the game for their alignment; examples of this can include mafia hardclaiming mafia without a strategic reason and/or revealing who the mafia team is in the thread.

[b]GOAT[/b] - Greatest of All Time[/hide]
[hide=H]

[b]Hammer[/b] - A vote that’s placed on a player which causes the wagon to reach majority in order to hang the player that the wagon is on, ending the day phase after the vote is placed.

[b]Hard Claim[/b] - to claim a role with there being no uncertain terms - see Soft Claim, is relevant.

[b]Hedging[/b] - When a player does not commit to a stance and stays on the fence regarding a topic, usually the alignment of a player.

[b]Hydra[/b] - An account shared by two or more players that share a role and have their own private chat.[/hide]
[hide=I]

[b]ISO[/b] - Isolating a specific player’s posts in order to look at all of them in one place.

[b]ITA[/b] - [b]In Thread Attack[/b] - A mechanic used in most mashes where every player has a X% hit rate shot they may use in the thread during designated times to attempt to kill someone.

As the stones walked left and right in the streets of the Box of Treasure, someone noticed a strange glint to a stone next to them. "Wait! Stop right there!" The purple shine of the stranger looked strangely familiar. As crowds began to form, the stones began murmuring among each other.
"I know who you are," a gemstone said as it walked over to the purple stone. "Amethyst. You don't belong here. We kicked you out months ago."
Everyone gasped, and the gemstone continued, "But since you came anyway, there's no leaving the Box."
There was a earth-shattering explosion, louder than the ones before. Smoke and dust was everywhere. And when everything cleared up, all there was left was shards of purple. "Purple quartz," one of the gemstones said disdainfully.
Everyone was silent for a moment. But they all knew what had happened. They had won. The Box of Treasure was pure once again.

tigeryong wrote:
Ahh i was debating baiting the n2 kill because it was 50/50 there was a roleblocker there.

Entro you played the newbcard quite well.

Baiting the n2 kill is always a mistake. If there is a roleblocker you are just dead.
If there is not a roleblocker, you prevent the game from being solved, as was what happened here. Preventing a single kill does not give town any more mishangs and it instead makes things more difficult for town. For example if panda was killed n2, then Mathbeast would be cleared, mathjay could claim and you could claim and then the game would be solved with 3 clears out of 5. But instead we have the scenario where Mathbeast is technically not cleared because there was no kill and you need 4 clears in order to solve the game.
